title: Insecure Cookie Configuration
description: One or more cookies are missing important security flags.
evidence: Cookies missing HttpOnly, Secure, or SameSite flags.
riskImpact: Cookies without proper security flags can be stolen via XSS attacks, sent over insecure connections, or exploited in CSRF attacks.
explanation: Cookie security flags provide essential protection: HttpOnly prevents JavaScript access, Secure ensures HTTPS-only, SameSite prevents CSRF.
fixSteps: Add HttpOnly flag to all session and authentication cookies.
Add Secure flag to ensure cookies are only sent over HTTPS.
Add SameSite=Lax or SameSite=Strict.

codeExamples.label: Secure cookie
codeExamples.code: response.cookies.set('session', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'lax' });

title: Cookie with Root Path
description: Cookie available to entire site via path=/.
evidence: Cookie path is root.
riskImpact: Cookie accessible from all paths on the domain.
explanation: Consider restricting cookie path for isolation.
fixSteps: Set specific path for cookies when appropriate.

title: Cookie with Broad Domain
description: Cookie set with leading dot domain (all subdomains).
evidence: Broad cookie domain.
riskImpact: Cookie shared across all subdomains.
explanation: Subdomain cookie sharing increases attack surface.
fixSteps: Restrict cookie domain unless sharing is needed.

title: Sensitive Cookie Missing __Host- Prefix
description: Session cookie could use __Host- prefix.
evidence: Cookie without __Host- prefix.
riskImpact: __Host- ensures cookie is secure, same-domain, path=/.
explanation: Cookie prefixes provide additional protections.
fixSteps: Rename sensitive cookies with __Host- prefix.

title: Third-Party Cookie Missing Partitioned
description: Third-party cookie missing Partitioned attribute.
evidence: Unpartitioned third-party cookie.
riskImpact: Cookie may be blocked in privacy-focused browsers.
explanation: CHIPS (Partitioned cookies) enable privacy-preserving third-party cookies.
fixSteps: Add Partitioned attribute for third-party cookies.

title: __Host- prefix without Secure
description: __Host- prefixed cookies MUST have Secure and Path=/.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-host-prefix-not-secure.
riskImpact: Add Secure; SameSite=...; Path=/ to the __Host- cookie
explanation: __Host- prefixed cookies MUST have Secure and Path=/.
fixSteps: Add Secure; SameSite=...; Path=/ to the __Host- cookie

title: __Host- prefix with non-/ Path
description: __Host- prefixed cookies MUST have Path=/.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-host-prefix-wrong-path.
riskImpact: Set Path=/ on the __Host- cookie
explanation: __Host- prefixed cookies MUST have Path=/.
fixSteps: Set Path=/ on the __Host- cookie

title: __Secure- prefix without Secure
description: __Secure- prefixed cookies MUST have the Secure attribute.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-secure-prefix-not-secure.
riskImpact: Add Secure to the __Secure- cookie
explanation: __Secure- prefixed cookies MUST have the Secure attribute.
fixSteps: Add Secure to the __Secure- cookie

title: Cookie Domain attribute is too loose
description: Cookies with an explicit Domain= attribute are sent to every subdomain. Avoid Domain= when not necessary.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-domain-set-too-loose.
riskImpact: Drop Domain= or scope to the required host only
explanation: Cookies with an explicit Domain= attribute are sent to every subdomain. Avoid Domain= when not necessary.
fixSteps: Drop Domain= or scope to the required host only

title: Cookie Expires/Max-Age > 1 year
description: Cookies that last more than a year violate some privacy regulations and live forever in browser storage.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-expires-too-far.
riskImpact: Limit cookie lifetime to 90 days for sessions, 1 year for persistent preferences
explanation: Cookies that last more than a year violate some privacy regulations and live forever in browser storage.
fixSteps: Limit cookie lifetime to 90 days for sessions, 1 year for persistent preferences

title: Cookie name leaks framework
description: Cookies named like express.sid, JSESSIONID, PHPSESSID, ASP.NET_SessionId reveal the server framework to attackers.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-name-disclosure.
riskImpact: Rename the cookie to a generic opaque name (e.g. __Host-id)
explanation: Cookies named like express.sid, JSESSIONID, PHPSESSID, ASP.NET_SessionId reveal the server framework to attackers.
fixSteps: Rename the cookie to a generic opaque name (e.g. __Host-id)

title: Session cookie without CSRF token
description: Session cookies should be paired with a CSRF token (double-submit cookie or synchronizer).
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-no-csrf-token.
riskImpact: Add a CSRF token (X-CSRF-Token header, hidden form field, or sync-token pattern)
explanation: Session cookies should be paired with a CSRF token (double-submit cookie or synchronizer).
fixSteps: Add a CSRF token (X-CSRF-Token header, hidden form field, or sync-token pattern)

title: Domain attribute without leading dot
description: RFC 6265bis deprecated the leading dot in the Domain attribute (e.g. Domain=example.com instead of Domain=.example.com). Modern user agents ignore the leading dot and treat both the same, so a missing dot is purely cosmetic but indicates the application is following older guidance.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-domain-no-leading-dot.
riskImpact: Remove the leading dot from Domain= attributes to align with RFC 6265bis and modern browser behavior.
explanation: RFC 6265bis deprecated the leading dot in the Domain attribute (e.g. Domain=example.com instead of Domain=.example.com). Modern user agents ignore the leading dot and treat both the same, so a missing dot is purely cosmetic but indicates the application is following older guidance.
fixSteps: Drop the leading dot from the Domain= attribute (use example.com, not .example.com).

title: Cookie Path crosses application boundary
description: A cookie Path=/ makes the cookie available to every route on the host, including unrelated apps served from subpaths (e.g. /admin, /api, /internal). This broadens cookie exposure across trust boundaries.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-path-cross-app.
riskImpact: Scope cookies to the smallest path needed (e.g. Path=/app, not Path=/) so unrelated apps cannot read them.
explanation: A cookie Path=/ makes the cookie available to every route on the host, including unrelated apps served from subpaths (e.g. /admin, /api, /internal). This broadens cookie exposure across trust boundaries.
fixSteps: Set the Path= attribute to the most specific route that requires the cookie.
Avoid Path=/ unless the cookie truly must be readable by every route.

title: Cookie Expires is in the past
description: Set-Cookie headers with an Expires value in the past cause immediate deletion by the user agent. This is sometimes intentional (logout) but often indicates a clock-skew bug or leftover debug cookie.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-expires-in-past.
riskImpact: Verify the past Expires is intentional; if it is, send Max-Age=0 instead and confirm it is only used on logout endpoints.
explanation: Set-Cookie headers with an Expires value in the past cause immediate deletion by the user agent. This is sometimes intentional (logout) but often indicates a clock-skew bug or leftover debug cookie.
fixSteps: Confirm the past Expires value is intentional (logout, deletion).
Prefer Max-Age=0 for explicit cookie deletion.
Audit cookies served from non-logout paths for stale dates.

title: Cookie Max-Age=0 deletion pattern
description: Max-Age=0 instructs the browser to delete the named cookie. This is the canonical logout / clear-cookie pattern. Repeated occurrences across the same scan usually indicate a buggy double-delete or a session cookie masquerading as a deletion.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-max-age-zero.
riskImpact: Limit Max-Age=0 Set-Cookie headers to logout endpoints; do not emit them on every authenticated request.
explanation: Max-Age=0 instructs the browser to delete the named cookie. This is the canonical logout / clear-cookie pattern. Repeated occurrences across the same scan usually indicate a buggy double-delete or a session cookie masquerading as a deletion.
fixSteps: Restrict Max-Age=0 Set-Cookie emission to logout / reset endpoints.
Verify the cookie being deleted is not the same one being set in the same response.

title: Cookie Domain=parent set on subdomain response
description: A cookie set by a subdomain (e.g. app.example.com) with Domain=example.com is broadcast to every other subdomain (admin.example.com, staging.example.com, dev.example.com). If any subdomain is compromised or co-tenanted, the attacker can read or forge the session.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-domain-parent-on-subdomain.
riskImpact: Drop Domain= on subdomain responses so the cookie becomes host-only; use __Host- prefix when possible.
explanation: A cookie set by a subdomain (e.g. app.example.com) with Domain=example.com is broadcast to every other subdomain (admin.example.com, staging.example.com, dev.example.com). If any subdomain is compromised or co-tenanted, the attacker can read or forge the session.
fixSteps: Remove the Domain= attribute so the cookie is host-only.
Rename sensitive cookies with the __Host- prefix to enforce host-only.
Audit co-tenanted subdomains and reduce blast radius.

title: Third-party cookie without SameSite
description: Cookies set in a third-party context (iframe embed, cross-site redirect) without SameSite=None will be blocked by modern browsers and will produce inconsistent behavior across browsers.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-no-samesite-third-party.
riskImpact: Set SameSite=None; Secure on cookies that are intentionally third-party; otherwise set SameSite=Lax or Strict.
explanation: Cookies set in a third-party context (iframe embed, cross-site redirect) without SameSite=None will be blocked by modern browsers and will produce inconsistent behavior across browsers.
fixSteps: If the cookie is intentionally cross-site, add SameSite=None; Secure.
If the cookie is not needed cross-site, add SameSite=Lax or Strict.
Audit embedded iframes and cross-site redirect chains for unnecessary third-party cookies.

title: Partitioned cookie missing Secure
description: Cookies carrying the Partitioned attribute (CHIPS) MUST also carry the Secure attribute, otherwise browsers will reject the cookie.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-partitioned-without-secure.
riskImpact: Add Secure alongside Partitioned on every Set-Cookie that uses the CHIPS attribute.
explanation: Cookies carrying the Partitioned attribute (CHIPS) MUST also carry the Secure attribute, otherwise browsers will reject the cookie.
fixSteps: Add Secure alongside Partitioned on every cookie that uses CHIPS.
Validate the host is HTTPS-served before adding Partitioned.

title: Cookie is host-only (no Domain attribute)
description: When Set-Cookie omits the Domain attribute, the cookie becomes host-only and is not sent to subdomains. This is the recommended setting per RFC 6265bis and the safest default.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-missing-domain-host-only.
riskImpact: Keep the cookie host-only; if subdomain sharing is required, explicitly add a scoped Domain= value.
explanation: When Set-Cookie omits the Domain attribute, the cookie becomes host-only and is not sent to subdomains. This is the recommended setting per RFC 6265bis and the safest default.
fixSteps: Confirm host-only behavior is intended.
For sensitive cookies, rename with the __Host- prefix to enforce host-only at the user-agent level.

title: Third-party cookie missing SameSite=None; Secure
description: Cross-site cookies MUST be issued with SameSite=None; Secure in order to be accepted by modern browsers. Lacking this combination, the cookie is silently dropped in cross-site contexts.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-third-party-no-samesite-none-secure.
riskImpact: Emit SameSite=None; Secure on every cookie that is required in a third-party (iframe / cross-site) context.
explanation: Cross-site cookies MUST be issued with SameSite=None; Secure in order to be accepted by modern browsers. Lacking this combination, the cookie is silently dropped in cross-site contexts.
fixSteps: Add SameSite=None and Secure to every cross-site cookie.
Verify the response is HTTPS (Secure requires HTTPS).

title: Cookie injection vector via __Host- / __Secure- prefix misuse
description: If an application lets user-controlled values (subdomain names, host headers, redirect targets) flow into a cookie name without validation, an attacker can craft a __Host- or __Secure- prefixed cookie name from a controlled subdomain, enabling fixation-style attacks against __Host- cookie issuance policies.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-host-prefix-injection-subdomain.
riskImpact: Reject cookie names that start with __Host- or __Secure- when the value is user-controlled; only the trusted server should set these prefixes.
explanation: If an application lets user-controlled values (subdomain names, host headers, redirect targets) flow into a cookie name without validation, an attacker can craft a __Host- or __Secure- prefixed cookie name from a controlled subdomain, enabling fixation-style attacks against __Host- cookie issuance policies.
fixSteps: Never let user input flow into the cookie name.
Hard-code __Host-/__Secure- cookie names on the trusted server only.
Audit subdomain takeover surface and co-tenanted subdomains.
